strengths: ~19 years of software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems, exceeding the 15-year minimum
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform used by Green Map, plus startup experience (WatchUp, RIDE Capital)
Current hands-on AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq, including MCP code generators and frameworks for agentic workflows
concerns: No director/principal-level scope: career history is senior/staff IC roles without evidence of driving org-wide technical strategy or advising strategic customers
No recognized AI expertise or demonstrated AI products/platforms at scale — AI exposure is recent (2025) and infrastructure-adjacent rather than product-defining
No Master's or PhD in an AI-related field; public speaking is limited to regional testing/GIS workshops rather than industry-level thought leadership
response.justification: The candidate clears the minimum bar: a BS in Computer Science and roughly 19 years of engineering experience spanning low-latency DLang microservices at Dunnhumby, AWS/TypeScript product work at Optilyz, and founding GISCollective, which satisfies the entrepreneurial preference. Current MCP/agentic-tooling work at re:cinq shows genuine but early-stage AI fluency. However, the role is a director-level AI strategy position requiring recognized AI expertise and demonstrated AI products or platforms; the resume shows no such recognition (talks are regional testing/GIS workshops, not AI thought leadership), no advanced AI degree, and no evidence of advising executives, customers, or setting cloud/AI product direction. Strong senior IC, but the strategic-leadership and AI-recognition competencies central to this role are absent.
